body_txt: Charlie (usually male, but not always) says or does something that can be reasonably interpreted as treating Alice unequally or unfairly due to her gender. However, when Charlie explains the actual motivation behind the preferential or discriminatory treatment, it's not a belief that Alice is less capable or deserving because of her gender. The true reason is far more benign and may range from being overly protective because: Compare Straw Misogynist where Charlie actually is sexist but with comically terrible reasoning and He-Man Woman Hater where Charlie is not only sexist but outright hates women. Compare Mistaken for Racist , where Charlie is believed to have a different kind of prejudice. Contrast Straw Feminist where Charlie is very pro-women but it is played for comedy or so their beliefs can be dramatically undercut. Contrast Female Misogynist where Charlie is a misogynist, but also happens to be female . Contrast the Noble Bigot who is sexist but views deferential treatment or exclusion of otherwise qualified women as chivalrous. May overlap with Big Brother Instinct and Always Save the Girl . Usually highlights a Double Standard . Not to Be Confused with Bait-and-Switch Accusation where Alice makes an accusation that turns out to not be what Charlie thinks he's being suspected of or Discriminate and Switch where Alice is being discriminated against but due to a different prejudice than they believe. No Real Life Examples, Please!
